deep_dive.title: On the Covalent Fields of Molecule-Surface Interactions
deep_dive.abstract: The ambiguity of the active site, the empirical status of Brønsted-Evans-Polanyi relations, and the unpredictability of linear scaling relation breakdown are three symptoms of a single representational choice: treating chemical affinity as an attribute of discrete geometric sites. Here we show that all three are resolved when chemical affinity is represented as a continuous property of the interface: the covalent field. We present a framework, Covalent Field Theory (CFT), in which active sites emerge as regions where the field sustains a bias toward bond formation beyond the thermal threshold, removing the need for geometric classification. Linear scaling relations are correlation structure in the field across probe families; their breakdown is a topological bifurcation with a precise geometric signature. Brønsted-Evans-Polanyi correlations arise from the covalent field decomposition, providing a theoretical basis for what has previously been treated as an empirical regularity, demonstrated across ~120,000 candidate pathways. Applied to a high-entropy alloy nanoparticle and a partially reduced high-entropy oxide, CFT maps these properties onto surfaces of arbitrary compositional and structural complexity.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Covalent Field Theory resolves active site ambiguity and scaling relation breakdowns for catalyst screening
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 共价场理论解决活性位点模糊性和标度关系崩溃，助力催化剂虚拟筛选
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 共价场理论解决活性位点模糊性和标度关系崩溃，助力催化剂虚拟筛选
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 共价场理论解决活性位点模糊性和标度关系崩溃，助力催化剂虚拟筛选
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes Covalent Field Theory where chemical affinity is a continuous field at interfaces, resolving active site ambiguity and linear scaling relation breakdowns, providing a novel representation for computational catalyst screening.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统催化理论中活性位点定义模糊、线性标度关系经验性强且时而崩溃的难题，该论文提出共价场理论，以连续界面场替代离散几何位点表示。该框架揭示了标度关系源自探针族间的相关结构，其崩溃对应拓扑分叉，从而为催化剂理性设计和反应条件虚拟筛选提供了统一、可预测的理论工具，并可直接融入密度泛函理论计算。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对传统催化理论中活性位点定义模糊、线性标度关系经验性强且时而崩溃的难题，该论文提出共价场理论，以连续界面场替代离散几何位点表示。该框架揭示了标度关系源自探针族间的相关结构，其崩溃对应拓扑分叉，从而为催化剂理性设计和反应条件虚拟筛选提供了统一、可预测的理论工具，并可直接融入密度泛函理论计算。

quick_skim.title: A green solvent screening tool for emerging materials via uncertainty aware, transformer enhanced transfer learning
quick_skim.abstract: Accurate prediction of solubility remains a central challenge across materials science and sustainable chemistry. In particular due to emerging technologies like organic and hybrid photovoltaics, batteries, and catalysis, solvent usage is expected to increase significantly within the coming years. Therefore, substituting solvents with greener alternatives is vital. This is where machine learning can have substantial impact. However, the limited data on critical parameters of solubility significantly constraints machine learning efficacy. In this work, we transfer a pre-trained foundational model on QM9 targets to our application with minimal data requirements. Additionally, the pipeline integrates uncertainty quantification, allowing the user to gauge the confidence of the predictions. As baseline, we succeed in predicting the Hansen solubility parameters and Dielectric Constant for which extensive databases exist. Importantly, we achieve high model performance on additional targets, such as Gutmann Donor and Acceptor numbers, where the available data is extremely limited. Overall, we augment data on solubility descriptors by orders of magnitude with high quality predictions. For effective dissemination, we deploy easy-to-use, easily integrateable with high throughput labs, customizable tool for ranking and screening possible solvent substitutes. Finally, we rediscovered known green solvent alternatives and proposed new candidates proving its relevance for finding eco-friendly solvents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: ML-based solvent screening tool for sustainable chemistry, directly applicable to virtual screening of reaction conditions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于机器学习的绿色溶剂筛选工具，直接适用于反应条件的虚拟筛选
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于机器学习的绿色溶剂筛选工具，直接适用于反应条件的虚拟筛选
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于机器学习的绿色溶剂筛选工具，直接适用于反应条件的虚拟筛选
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work presents a ML tool for green solvent screening via transfer learning and uncertainty quantification, enabling computational virtual screening of reaction conditions for organic synthesis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本研究针对新材料领域绿色溶剂筛选需求，提出基于迁移学习和不确定性量化的溶解度预测工具。该方法在数据稀缺情况下仍能准确预测，并量化不确定性，为有机合成中反应溶剂的虚拟筛选提供了可行的计算解决方案，有助于加速绿色化学工艺的开发。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本研究针对新材料领域绿色溶剂筛选需求，提出基于迁移学习和不确定性量化的溶解度预测工具。该方法在数据稀缺情况下仍能准确预测，并量化不确定性，为有机合成中反应溶剂的虚拟筛选提供了可行的计算解决方案，有助于加速绿色化学工艺的开发。
